title: Function reference
excerpt:

posts.author: phawley (Peter Hawley)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have been searching for a better function reference than the one in the linked documentation in Monday.com Formula Column. Does one exist somewhere?

The one in the documentation is 7 years old and is incomplete. Other functions are available to perform calculations, but they are not documented anywhere that I can find. The VALUE and ISNUMBER are not documented.

I have also noticed several things regarding the available documentation:

  • Incomplete TOC - the TOC is missing several sub-headings.
  • Function order - the individual function areas should be presented in alphabetical order within their sections.
  • Parameter explanations - every parameter needs to be identified and explained fully.

I feel that this is the sort of shoddy workmanship that will keep your product out of the mainstream.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @phawley

understand your concerns regarding the monday.com Formula Column documentation. Have you used the new AI formula builder? I have found it super helpful. It gives me suggestions on how to achieve what I’m looking for.

image719×431 31.7 KB

As of now, the official resources include:

  • Available Functions in the Formula Column: This article lists the functions available but may not cover all, such as VALUE and ISNUMBER.

Monday Support

  • The Formula Column: Provides an overview of how to use the Formula Column, including some functions and examples.

Monday Support

  • Formula Use Cases: Offers practical examples of formulas in action, which might help uncover undocumented functions.

Monday Support

Despite these resources, it’s clear that the documentation may not be comprehensive or up-to-date. Community discussions, such as the one you’ve referenced, highlight similar frustrations.

monday Community Forum

For additional insights, you might find the following video helpful: https://youtu.be/ie4xMrChfsc
